context.summary: Show how hot or cold your micro:bit is using the built-in temperature sensor.
how_it_works: This program shows how hot or cold your micro:bit is by taking a reading from the temperature sensor in its processor or CPU (central processing unit).
The processorâs temperature is a fairly good approximation of the temperature around you in Â°C (Celsius).
In this program, when you press input button A, the micro:bit displays the processorâs current temperature on its LED display output.
Take the micro:bit into warmer and colder places and see how the temperature readings change.
improve_it: Compare the reading with another thermometer. How accurate is the micro:bit? Do you need to modify the micro:bit reading to get the air temperature?
Convert the temperature to Fahrenheit or Kelvin.
Use radio to make a remote sensor sending temperature readings to another micro:bit, for example from outside to inside. You could make an indoor / outdoor thermometer this way.
